purpose: Define, document, and enforce how per-trade position size is determined. Size must be bounded, explainable from first principles, and respect bankroll health, concurrency caps, liquidity constraints, and confidence tiers. If the current sizing logic already meets these requirements, this task produces documentation proving it. If gaps exist (found by phase 0 audit), this task closes them.

in_scope: core/paper_exec.py — sizing logic, open_position(), how qty is determined
core/risk.py — any sizing-related gates in check_entry_gates() (min/max size constraints, depth checks)
out_of_scope: .env — do not change threshold values. Read only. Document what values are live.
mlb_model/ — do not touch. Model confidence output is an input to sizing, not redesigned here.
Do not redesign confidence scoring or model logic
Do not change TP/SL thresholds — those belong to phase 1

sizing_invariants_to_enforce.description: The following rules must hold after this phase. If they already hold, document that they do. If they do not, fix them.

rules.description: No single trade may commit more than MAX_POSITION_USD. Value must come from env var or hardcoded constant, not derived dynamically in a way that can be gamed.
rules.verify: Show MAX_POSITION_USD (or equivalent) cap in paper_exec.py

rules.description: Total capital committed across all open trades must not exceed a configured cap. If this cap does not exist, add it or document the gap as a HIGH severity finding.
rules.verify: Show where open_count and committed_usd are checked before opening a new position

rules.description: Higher confidence → larger size, subject to caps. The tiers or formula must be explicit and documented.
rules.verify: Show the confidence-to-size formula or tier table verbatim from code

rules.description: Size must not exceed available depth in the orderbook. MIN_TOUCH_DEPTH_USD gate must be checked before sizing, not after.
rules.verify: Show where depth check gates sizing

rules.description: Given entry confidence C, bankroll B, open positions N, and depth D — the resulting qty must be derivable by a human following a documented formula. No magic numbers.
rules.verify: Document the formula with variable names matching the code

rules.description: qty * entry_px must equal committed_usd within floating point tolerance. Verify this is enforced at open_position() call site.
rules.verify: Show the assignment and any rounding logic

result_must_include: sizing_formula: verbatim from code (or newly written)
caps_enforced: list of cap rules confirmed present
gaps_closed: list of gaps from audit report that this phase fixed
gaps_remaining: list of gaps not addressed in this phase (with justification)
verification: py_compile passes for all changed files
sizing_rules_spec_must_include: Canonical sizing formula with variable definitions
Confidence tier table (if tiered) or formula (if continuous)
All size caps (per-trade, per-session, per-market) with env var names and current values
Example: given confidence=0.65, bankroll=$900, 1 open position, depth=$200 — what is the resulting size?

preflight_questions: What does the phase 0 audit say about current sizing logic gaps?
What is the current MAX_POSITION_USD (or equivalent) value from .env?
Is committed_usd already tracked correctly in open_position()?
Is there currently a per-session total commitment cap? (Check phase 0 audit findings.)
acceptance_criteria: All 6 sizing invariants above are either confirmed present or fixed
Sizing formula is documented verbatim with variable names matching code
Only files in allowed_files were changed
No threshold values were changed without explicit manager approval
proof_of_work: Show git diff for each changed file
Show the worked example from SIZING_RULES_SPEC_001.md (confidence=0.65, bankroll=$900, 1 open position, depth=$200)
For each cap rule, show the exact code line that enforces it
guardrails: Do not redesign strategy or model logic
Do not reintroduce ambiguous YES/NO economics
Held-side contract truth must remain the basis for pricing and exit logic
Do not widen scope to bot_core.py or dashboard
